title: Crispy Japanese Chicken Skewers
description: Peppery, caramelbraised chicken cubes on skewers, baked to a golden crisp and finished with a sweet glaze-perfect for a lazy Sunday lunch.
instructions: In a bowl, whisk sesame oil, soy sauce, mirin, rice vinegar, grated ginger, minced garlic, honey, Sriracha, sea salt, and black pepper.
Add chicken cubes to the bowl, tossing to coat evenly. Marinate in the refrigerator for 30 minutes.
Soak bamboo skewers in water for 30 minutes or use metal skewers if preferred. After marination, thread the chicken onto skewers, leaving a small space between pieces.
Arrange skewers on a parchment‑lined baking sheet, spacing them so heat can circulate.
Bake for 15 minutes, then flip and bake for an additional 12–15 minutes until the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C) and the surface is golden brown.
While the skewers bake, make a caramel glaze: melt 2 tbsp butter in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add the honey to the butter and stir until the mixture turns a light amber and becomes syrupy, about 3 minutes.
Remove from heat, add 1 tbsp soy sauce to deglaze, whisk until combined. Let cool slightly.
Remove baked skewers from the oven and drizzle the caramel glaze over them. Return to the oven for 2 minutes to set the glaze.
Optional: broil the skewers for 1–2 minutes to crisp the edges further. Remove and let rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve hot as a tasty, low‑carb lunch or snack.
